text: Hello friends, this is Dr. Arjun Pawar, consultant pediatric surgeon from Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar.
In this video, we are going to watch thoracoscopic CDH repair in a 3 day old neonate.
We are able to see large bowel loops and small bowel loops within the thoracic cavity.

now we have entered into the abdominal cavity through the defect these are the small bowel and large bowel loops we are able to see the defect now we will insert the ports under vision now we will try to reduce the thoracic contents gradually into the abdominal cavity through the defect in the diaphragm
Care should be taken, reduction should be done gradually, steadily.
We should not grasp the bowel with the forceps or instruments to avoid injury to the bowel.
Nemo insufflation will help to reduce the contents easily.
We have achieved complete reduction of the contents.
Now we are able to see the diaphragmatic defect.
There is evidence of posterolateral defect into the diaphragm that is a classical Bogdallic hernia.
there is a spleen even after complete reduction the abdominal contents will try to come into the thoracic cavity repeatedly we have to push it again
For the ease of closure we will try to mobilize the posterior leaflet.
The mobilization of this leaflet will help during suturing.
This is the stomach with IFT in situ.
Posterolateral wall of the diaphragm dissected adequately.

after complete reduction of the contents and adequate mobilization of the leaflets of the diaphragm we will start suturing the defect in this case we are going to do a extra corporeal suturing with knot pushing technique particularly we are using modified melzer's knot
After pushing the knot inside the thoracic cavity, we will tie the knot.
After that, we will put additional three to four intracorporeal knots to secure the knots properly.
the advantage of extracorporeal measures knot is that it is more secure even if the leaflets of the diaphragm are under tension it holds them properly and prevent the knot slippage again
After securing the malus knot, now we are taking few more intracorporeal knots to secure it more properly.
This technique also increases the speed of surgery as it is less time consuming.
During this surgery, after initial placement of the port and reduction of the contents, usually we remove the both working ports and we pass the needle directly through the port side wound.

this will aid in the easy in and out movement of the instrument and needle and also will avoid the excess pneumatic pressure within the thoracic cavity so that the physiological parameters are not deranged too much in this particular case we have placed all extra corporal knots
The modified measures knot is particularly more useful in this lateral most part of the defect where the edges of the defect are further and under more tension.
The little most part of the defect is closed by a rib anchoring stitch.
We pass the needle through both the leaflets and pull out the needle using suture passer needle.
Secure the knot outside the rib and bury the knot in subcutaneous tissue.
this we have taken one more knot at the middle end of the defect this is how we have completed the suturing of the diaphragmatic defect
at the end of the procedure we are able to see well inflated lung upper lobe and lower lobe of the left lung thank you so much for watching the video till the end
